Key Takeaways:
• In the last video we talked about the “Hyper” Seed Launch and mentioned there
were two ways to approach it. The first is the “Product Buildout”. The second is…
• #2: “Show Me the Money”
o This method is more complicated and harder to pull off - but builds your
list and makes you money as you do it
o People have built serious businesses from scratch using this model
o It’s possible to mess this up by being overly manipulative - if used
incorrectly it could end up burning bridges and killing the business
o In some ways similar to the Product Buildout model - line up experts that
are willing to be interviewed
o Each expert gets the opportunity to pitch their product at the end of the
interview (the last 10-15 minutes). The pitch is usually made with an
affiliate link - you get a commission from the sales
o Let the expert know this is part of a series - have around 5 parts/experts
o Use the social proof that other experts will also be on this interview series
o This can backfire if you name drop but haven’t gotten confirmation from
the experts you’ve invited yet - be careful here. If you can get 1-2 experts to
say yes it will be easier to get others on board
• Important Note: Ask the expert to email their list about the webinar or teleseminar,
then have people join your list to register. As you do this you build your list with
each call
• To get the expert on board use strategies similar to when you’re building
relationships with JV partners. Each time you do an interview email your own list
about the event. Do the call and allow them to pitch at the end
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 2 of 8 --

3
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
• You could also make it even more attractive by not taking a cut of sales (this is
optional and negotiable). Make sure to follow up after the call with a replay. Then
follow up with an email about the offer - make sure there is a deadline (scarcity)
• The toughest part of this model is getting the first expert to participate and mail
their list. After that, you’re rolling
• You’ll want to use teleseminars and webinar tools for this
• You want the calls to be interactive - need ability to mute and unmute individual
lines
• For Teleseminars:
o Instant Seminar - has muting feature
o GotoWebinar - has muting feature
o Voice Text - doesn’t have muting feature
o Freeconferencecall.com - may not have feature
• Webinars:
o Instant Teleseminar - has muting feature
o GotoWebinar - has muting feature
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 3 of 8 --

4
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
Now we're continuing on with the Seed Launch. Well, there are two ways to do
this. We just talked about the Product Build Out. Now we're going to talk about the
Show Me The Money Method. This one is more complicated and it's harder to pull
off, but it really does build your list and it makes you money at the same time. I've
literally seen people build serious businesses from scratch by following this model.
I'm talking about six figure businesses within the first year. It's possible to screw
this up, because I've seen some people do this and be overly manipulative. When they
did that, they ended up burning some bridges and really killing their business by doing
that. This is the one where it's possible for there to be shenanigans involved. You want to
avoid that and be ethical about this.
It's starts off similar to the Product Build Out and then you line up a series of
experts who are willing to be interviewed on a teleseminar or a webinar. The way they do
this is they hopefully will offer 60 minutes of content and then pitch their product at the
end of the call. They take 10 or 15 minutes to pitch their product at the end of the call.
Usually the pitch is made through an affiliate link, so you get a cut of the sales. As
you're doing this, you're making money from affiliate sales. It's beautiful, because you
have the expert on your call and they're actually providing the content and you're getting
paid an affiliate commission for any products that are sold.
When you're lining up the experts, you want to let them know it's part of a series
that you're doing. If you do something like this, you probably want to do six calls or you
could do something like four calls. You probably want to do six or eight calls.
You let the experts know that they are doing it as part of a series. One of the
things you can do and this is where the potential sliminess comes into play, is use social
proof of other experts. Other experts are going to be on this webinar or teleseminar
series. Having experts will attract other experts. I
I've seen this backfire is where people went and they invited a bunch of experts.
They didn't get a yes from any of the experts. They had Guru A, B, C, and D. They sent
invitations to Guru A and B and C. They put photos of Guru A, B, and C, as if all those
gurus had already said yes. And they did it as a social proof way to get the other gurus on
board. You are walking a fine line of sliminess.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 4 of 8 --

5
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
You could just say, “These are the ones I've invited.” And as soon as you get one
to say yes, then you can say, “Hey, Guru A said yes. I'd love to have you on, because it's
going to be a really great series.”
You can use social proof. It's like a domino thing. If you can get one person to say
yes, then you can use the social proof of that one person to get another person. Then you
can walk forward doing that. If you can get one or two experts to say yes, then you can
usually line up six or eight of them really easily.
If you have six or eight gurus lined up and they all have lists, now the key is to get
them to mail their lists. They're always going to be happy mailing their lists, but again,
you can use the social proof routine that the others gurus agreed to mail and they're
going to mail.
The other thing is if each guru mails for that webinar or teleseminar, now you're
building your list with each call. If you have 200 or 500 or 1,000 people with each
webinar, you can see how you can build a list very quickly. Then what starts to happen is
this, say you're going to have six calls and the first call you put 200 people on your list.
Now you have 200 people and now you go to your second call, Guru B mails and
you get 200 more people on the list, but you had the original 200. Now you're mailing all
400 and telling them about this new call, this webinar that's coming up. Now you have
400 people on the line and you have 400 people you can potentially sell when you make
your offer at the end of the call.
Then Guru C, you're going to interview and he emails and he puts 200 more
people on your list. Now you have 600 people that you're mailing and telling them about
your call. You can see how you can walk forward and how each time gets more powerful.
As you start to build your list, then when you're lining up more gurus, maybe you're into
your seventh week now and you can say that you've already done six calls and you have
1,200 people on your list.
For the seventh call, you can say, “Hey, we have this list of 1,200 people, we're
going to be mailing them and we're going to be putting them on the call, as well.” You
can see it becomes a snowball and it gets bigger and bigger and bigger. I've seen this over
and over. It's worked very effectively.
The toughest thing is getting that first guru to say yes and to mail their list. That's
the toughest part. How do you reach them? You do it sort of like the same way I talked
about in the JV training, the way you reach a JV. You can attend an event where they're
speaking. You can attend their event. You can follow them on Facebook and Twitter. You
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 5 of 8 --

6
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
can post comments on their blog. You can buy their products. You can sell their products
as an affiliate.
What you want to do is pick out a handful of targets and then work very hard to
get on their radar. Then eventually ask them for the interview and ask them to mail their
list for the interview, as well.
Each time you do one of these, you're emailing your list about the next
teleseminar or webinar. You pitch at the end of the call. You can make it more attractive
if you allow them to pitch and you say, “I don't even want an affiliate commission, you
can keep 100%.” That's your decision on that. Maybe some of the times you do that and
maybe some of the times you take a commission.
Everything is a negotiation. Maybe the first time or the first couple times, when
you're still building up that social proof. Maybe you don't take a cut as an affiliate. Maybe
you change it going forward.
After you do the call, you have to follow up with the replay. You have to email
your list and send them to a link where they can listen to the replay. Then you need to
follow up with an email about the offer, basically reiterating the offer that was made in
the teleseminar. Make sure there's a deadline, so people have a reason to jump on board,
a reason to act. A deadline always gets them to move.
The toughest part, as I mentioned earlier, is getting the first expert to participate
and to mail his or her list. Once you get the first couple in place, they fall like dominoes
and this gets really easy. This is harder to do in the internet marketing world, although
I've seen it done at least two or three times.
I've also seen it done in other niches. Generally, in other niches outside of
internet marketing, it's ten times easier, because the experts aren't getting approached to
do interviews all the time. In fact, they're probably almost never getting approached to
do interviews.
I've seen this work really effectively in all kinds of niche markets and it's much
easier to do in those markets.
This is the Show Me The Money Seed Launch and it is incredibly powerful.
Like I said, it's not the easiest thing in the world to do. It takes some work, some
hustling, and some negotiation. You have to get out there and talk to people, whether it's
on the phone or at live events. Once you line up two or three experts, you are in the
money.

7
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
It's extremely doable.
I have to wrap up and give you a couple of resources. I borrowed this portion
from the Prelaunch content tools. Mind map. You really want these calls to be
interactive. I think you do want them to be interactive.
If you want them to be interactive, you need to have the ability to mute and
unmute individual lines. Even if you have 10 or 15 people on the call, if you just try to
open up the entire line …. the way the bridge lines work is you can mute the line or you
can unmute it.
If you mute it, then people are only going to be able to hear you and any other
presenters, anyone who calls in through the presenter line, with the presenter pin code.
That's the way they work. As a presenter, you can mute the line, where they can only hear
you. Or you can unmute the line and then everyone can hear everyone else.
When you unmute the lines, it's absolute chaos. Someone's going to be yelling at
their kid, someone is going to be flushing the toilet, someone's going to be having a
conversation with someone else. You're going to hear all that. Even if there's only 10 or
20 people on the line, it's going to be very noisy and you get to hear every cough and
sneeze, everything going on in the background. You don't want that.
There are services that will allow you to mute and unmute individual lines. If
someone has a special code, like a star 1 or a star 2, it will “raise their hand.” And you're
going to be able to see that – their “raising their hand.” Then you can individually
unmute their line, so you're not getting a lot of extra noise in the background. You
definitely want that capability if you're going to have any kind of interactivity at all.
You can use Instant Teleseminar and GotoWebinar. They both have that
capability, to unmute lines individually. If you go with Voice Text, I'm not sure if you'll
have that.
They can probably provide that capability and it will probably be more expensive.
FreeConferenceCall.com didn't use to, but they might be doing it now. I heard a rumor
that it was coming, so it might be there.
There are lots of other people that will provide bridge lines, but remember to ask
about that ability to mute and unmute individual lines.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

With webinars, the primary ones that I know about are Instant Teleseminar and
GotoWebinar and both of those give you the capability to mute and unmute individual
lines.
That's it for the Seed Launch. Again this is a really powerful way to build a
product. It's the easiest and fastest way to build a product. You can do it with a very
small list. Once you have a product, you have a lot more power. You can potentially
attract joint venture partners and affiliates.
Then, of course, if you're up to the task that Hyper Seed Launch is super
powerful. It's a great way to start from scratch. It's not the easiest. You need to hustle a
bit and you need to be able to talk to people, negotiate with people, and convince them to
come on with you.
